why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound minecraft:transfer must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a body-consuming transfer decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence for one official host/port fixture only and is separate from server transfer UX, reconnect behavior, network reconnection, Configuration completion, Play entry, world load, render readiness, or runtime behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_transfer_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 11, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x0b.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ClientboundTransferPacket(String, int), ClientboundTransferPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundTransferPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and ClientboundTransferPacket field accessors.
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound transfer dispatch/decode.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound mapping contains store_cookie id 0x0a, but not transfer id 0x0b.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0xb in Clientbound Configuration; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Configuration clientbound transfer packet id 11
